label: Weakest
short_description: Outcomes measured at one point in time, or before-and-after with no comparison group

label: Weak
short_description: Outcomes measured before and after intervention with a non-equivalent comparison group
design_examples: Pre-post with comparator; matched cohort without rigorous matching
causal_inference: Limited; selection bias and time effects confounded

label: Moderate
short_description: Outcomes measured in multiple treatment and comparison units, with comparison units selected to be similar
design_examples: Multi-site comparison; basic propensity-score matching; cohort study with covariate adjustment
causal_inference: Plausible if comparison group is well-matched
typical_uses: Mid-stage evaluation when randomisation is infeasible

label: Strong
short_description: Comparison between treatment and comparison units accounting for unobservable differences
design_examples: Difference-in-differences; regression discontinuity; instrumental variables; interrupted time series; synthetic control

label: Strongest
short_description: Random assignment of units to treatment and control conditions
design_examples: Randomised controlled trial; cluster RCT; stepped-wedge RCT
typical_uses: Pilot evaluation, programme that can be randomised
